---
title: "UIAO_115 Performance Engineering — Kickoff Plan"
subtitle: "Defer-exit criteria, baseline measurement infrastructure, and profile-then-optimize sequencing"
date: "2026-05-10"
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"
description: "Project-plan companion to the §4.4 assessment that deferred UIAO_115. Defines the empirical baseline that must exist before any optimization work begins, the criteria for promoting UIAO_115 from ⚠️ aspirational / deferred to 🟡 implementation-active, and the profile-driven sequencing once the criteria are met."
---

## Plan metadata

| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Program | UIAO_127 (Project Plans) |
| Template | UIAO_127 Project Plan (kickoff shape) |
| Subject | UIAO_115 — Runtime Optimization & Performance Engineering Layer |
| Plan version | 1.0 (first delivery) |
| Closes | UIAO_115 Phase 2 follow-up from the [§4.4 assessment](2026-04-26-ha-perf-recovery-tenancy-assessment.qmd) |
| Companion | [UIAO_114 HA Kickoff Plan](2026-05-10-uiao_114-ha-kickoff-plan.qmd) (shares baseline infrastructure B1) |

## Purpose

The §4.4 assessment correctly observed that performance work without
empirical baseline is premature: the spec lists optimization techniques
(Numba/Rust kernels, Redis cache, M365 delta tokens, vectorized rule
evaluation) but does not bind them to measured baselines. This plan
makes the deferral **operational** by naming three things explicitly:

1. **Defer-exit criteria** — the empirical signals that say
   "optimization work is now warranted because a real hotspot is
   measured against a real baseline."
2. **Baseline measurement infrastructure** — what must ship *first*
   so the optimization work can be reviewed against actual hot paths
   rather than speculative bottlenecks.
3. **Profile-then-optimize sequencing** — the implementation order
   once the criteria fire. Crucially, this sequencing is **driven
   by the baseline**, not by the spec's section order. The spec's
   §3 (KSI eval), §4 (drift), §5 (OSCAL) are presented as parallel
   optimization domains; in practice we will optimize whichever the
   baseline measures as the actual hotspot first.

This is a kickoff plan, not an implementation plan. No optimization
code lands in this PR.

## Defer-exit criteria

UIAO_115 leaves "deferred" status when **any one** of the following
fires (logical-OR, not AND — a single concrete bottleneck is enough
to start work). Until then, substrate-status continues to show
UIAO_115 as ⚠️ aspirational with a "deferred pending baseline" note.

### C1 — A measured hotspot exceeds a stated SLO

The substrate's pipeline timing surface (B1 below) records 30
consecutive days of latency where one or more planes consistently
exceeds the spec's per-plane budget:

- Plane 1 (Ingestion) > 3s p95
- Plane 2 (IR + KSI) > 1s p95
- Plane 3 (Evidence Bundles) > 2s p95
- Plane 4 (OSCAL) > 2s p95
- Total > 10s p95

**Measurement:** automated alert from B1 reporting; not operator
intuition.

### C2 — A real tenant request exceeds platform-team SLO contract

If/when a platform team is named (per the [HA kickoff plan](2026-05-10-uiao_114-ha-kickoff-plan.qmd)
C3) and contracts an SLO with a regulated tenant, breach of that SLO
fires C2 even if the spec's own per-plane budgets aren't exceeded.

**Measurement:** SLO contract + breach event in the platform team's
paging system.

### C3 — Cost growth outstrips evidence growth

Substrate compute cost per evidence bundle climbs above a
to-be-defined threshold (a 2× cost-per-bundle increase year-over-year
without a 2× bundle-volume increase). C3 is the cost-driven trigger
when latency itself is acceptable but the substrate is becoming
expensive to run.

**Measurement:** quarterly cost-per-bundle calculation from the
platform team's cost reporting + B1 bundle-volume data.

## Baseline measurement infrastructure (ships first)

The §4.4 assessment specifically called out that timing measurements
exist (`PlaneResult` / `AdapterRun` dataclasses record wall-clock
duration) but SLO enforcement does not. The baseline infrastructure
turns those raw measurements into a queryable, alertable surface.

### B1 — Per-pipeline timing surface (shared with UIAO_114 K3)

Same deliverable as the [HA kickoff plan B1](2026-05-10-uiao_114-ha-kickoff-plan.qmd#b1-per-pipeline-timing-surface-extends-uiao_115-work)
— extends `PlaneResult` / `AdapterRun` into a structured
`PipelineTimingReport` API:

- Per-run, per-plane latency in canonical claim form.
- Per-adapter dispatch latency.
- Per-OSCAL-emission latency, with the artifact size as a paired
  metric (cost-per-byte is the right ratio for §3.4 short-circuiting
  decisions).
- Queryable via Auditor API with a 90-day rolling window.

B1 is shared with UIAO_114; whichever kickoff begins first ships B1.

### B2 — Cache-effectiveness telemetry

The spec's §6 names four cache layers (IR / KSI / Evidence Bundle /
OSCAL). None of them is currently implemented; B2 is **not building
the caches** — it is instrumenting the points where caches *would*
sit so we can measure the effective hit rate before deciding which
layer is worth implementing.

Concretely:

- Hash the deterministic cache keys at every cache boundary.
- Log a "would-have-hit" signal whenever the same cache key is
  computed twice within a configurable window (default: 1 hour).
- Aggregate hit-rate per cache layer per pipeline run.

Output: a 90-day report showing which cache layer would have the
highest payoff. Optimization work then prioritizes that layer.

### B3 — Adapter-evidence delta tracking

The spec's §2.4 calls for incremental ingestion (M365 delta tokens,
Defender deltas). B3 instruments the existing snapshot-based
adapters to log how much of each snapshot is **unchanged** from the
previous run. High unchanged-percentage is the signal that delta
ingestion will pay off.

Output: per-adapter delta-eligibility score in B1's reporting
surface. Adapters scoring high are first candidates for §2.4
implementation.

## Profile-then-optimize sequencing (after defer-exit fires)

Once any C1/C2/C3 fires and B1–B3 have shipped, optimization work
proceeds in this order, **driven by the baseline data**, not by
spec section order.

### Phase 2A — Read the baseline and pick the target (~2 days)

- Pull 30 days of B1 + B2 + B3 data.
- Identify the single highest-leverage optimization (likely either
  the cache layer with the highest would-have-hit rate, or the
  adapter with the highest delta-eligibility score).
- File a Phase-2B project plan naming the chosen target, the
  baseline that justifies it, and the SLO improvement target.

### Phase 2B — Ship the chosen optimization (~2-4 weeks per target)

- Implementation per the spec section that covers the chosen target.
- Re-run B1 against the same evidence set; quantify the SLO
  improvement.
- If the improvement meets target: ship + close the project plan.
- If the improvement misses target: file a follow-up plan; do not
  stack additional optimizations on the same hot path until the
  current one is understood.

### Phase 2C — Repeat for the next-highest hotspot

- Loop back to Phase 2A. Each cycle is one optimization at a time.

The discipline: never optimize without a measured target, and never
stack optimizations without measuring each in isolation. This is
deliberately slow compared to "implement the spec end-to-end"; the
slowness is the point. Optimization without baselines produces
unreviewable diffs.

## Anti-patterns this plan blocks

These approaches were considered and rejected:

1. **"Implement the spec front-to-back."** The spec's §3.3 calls
   for Numba/Rust kernels for vectorized KSI evaluation. Without
   knowing whether KSI eval is even a hotspot, this would be
   speculative complexity.
2. **"Add Redis."** The spec mentions Redis for caching. Caching is
   the right pattern when measured hit rate justifies it; B2 measures
   that. Adding Redis without B2 data couples the substrate to a
   Redis dependency that may add no value.
3. **"Profile in development against synthetic data."** Synthetic
   benchmarks routinely mislead. The defer-exit criteria above
   require *production* data because that is what optimization
   needs to honor.

## Action items

| # | Action | Owner | Due |
|---|---|---|---|
| 115.K1 | Land this plan; substrate-status §"Open backlog" links to it under Tier 2 item 6 | Substrate maintainer | This PR |
| 115.K2 | B1 (per-pipeline timing surface) — design + ship; shared with UIAO_114 | Substrate maintainer | Before C1 can fire |
| 115.K3 | B2 (cache-effectiveness telemetry) — design + ship | Substrate maintainer | After B1 |
| 115.K4 | B3 (adapter delta tracking) — design + ship | Substrate maintainer | After B1 |
| 115.K5 | Once defer-exit fires, file Phase-2A plan with the chosen optimization target | Substrate maintainer + platform team (if named) | After exit fires |

## Status reporting contract

Until the kickoff fires, substrate-status §"Open backlog" Tier 2 item
6 is the canonical status surface. Annotation:

> **UIAO_115 Performance Engineering** — deferred pending baseline
> per the 2026-04-26 §4.4 assessment. Defer-exit criteria and
> baseline measurement infrastructure documented in the
> [2026-05-10 kickoff plan](2026-05-10-uiao_115-perf-kickoff-plan.qmd).
> No optimization work in flight; profile-then-optimize discipline
> in force.

## Related documents

- [§4.4 Assessment — UIAO_114 / 115 / 117 / 119](2026-04-26-ha-perf-recovery-tenancy-assessment.qmd)
- [UIAO_114 HA Kickoff Plan](2026-05-10-uiao_114-ha-kickoff-plan.qmd)
- Canon source: [`src/uiao/canon/specs/performance.md`](https://github.com/WhalerMike/uiao/blob/main/src/uiao/canon/specs/performance.md)
